description:
GOAL: Cooperative scheduler with processes-as-continuations. Single OS
thread for v1; multi-thread is a near-term follow-up (user, 2026-05-11).

## Decisions captured (from design discussion)

- ONE shared address space across processes (NOT BEAM-style separate
  spaces). Pointers valid across process boundaries.
- Per-process heap chosen specifically for BOUNDED GC PAUSES: each
  process's heap is GC'd independently; other processes keep running.
- CPS codegen (from .11) makes processes trivial: a process IS a
  continuation pointer + a per-process heap. No stackful coroutines,
  no corosensei, no fiber stacks. Switching processes = update which
  continuation the trampoline drives next.
- Cooperative: yields at receive (.19.3) and at GC safepoints
  (.11.31). Optional explicit yield builtin can come later.
- Single OS thread for v1. Multi-thread / work-stealing follow-up.

## Prerequisites (resolve first; this ticket consumes their output)

- fz-ul4.11.32 — Per-Process runtime context (de-TLS). Process struct
  exists, heap + map_builder + jit_ctx live on it, every FFI fn takes
  `ctx: *mut Process`. THIS TICKET BUILDS ON THAT.

After .11.32 lands, this ticket adds the scheduler-level fields (pid,
mailbox, state, next_frame) onto the existing Process struct and builds
the Scheduler around it. The heavy mechanical FFI migration is .11.32's
job; this ticket is scheduler logic.

## Deliverables (new src/runtime/process.rs and src/runtime/scheduler.rs)

```rust
pub struct Process {
    // from .11.32:
    pub heap: Heap,
    pub map_builder: MapBuilder,
    pub jit_ctx: Option<JitCtx>,
    // new here:
    pub pid: PidId,
    pub next_frame: *mut u8,     // current continuation; null = exited
    pub mailbox: VecDeque<FzValue>,  // .19.3 populates
    pub state: State,            // Ready / Blocked(receive) / Exited
}

pub struct Scheduler {
    procs: HashMap<PidId, Process>,
    run_queue: VecDeque<PidId>,
    next_pid: u32,
}

impl Scheduler {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    pub fn spawn_initial(&mut self, entry_frame: *mut u8) -> PidId;
    pub fn run_until_idle(&mut self);  // drives trampoline across ready procs
    pub fn step(&mut self, pid: PidId);  // one quantum for one process
}
```

The trampoline (post-.11.32 it takes `&mut Process`) is now driven by
Scheduler: pick a Ready process, run it until it yields (receive,
explicit yield, halt). The scheduler picks the next ready process.

## Yield mechanism

- HALT (process exits): trampoline returns null; scheduler removes
  process.
- RECEIVE BLOCK (.19.3): trampoline calls a `fz_receive(ctx)` builtin;
  if mailbox empty, builtin sets process.state = Blocked, returns a
  sentinel that signals the trampoline to yield. Scheduler doesn't
  re-enqueue until send wakes it.
- GC SAFEPOINT (.11.31): GC pause itself doesn't yield to OTHER
  processes (it's per-process); but document that we COULD yield to
  another process at safepoints in a future preemption design.
- COOPERATIVE YIELD (optional v1): a `yield()` builtin that returns
  control to the scheduler without blocking. Decide at impl time
  whether to expose now or defer.

## Tests

- Spawn 3 processes, each running a counter loop; verify all make
  progress (cooperative round-robin).
- A process that exits (returns Halt) is removed from scheduler.
- Single-process degenerate case: existing JIT tests still work
  (one process named "main"; SAMPLE-based tests pass via the
  Scheduler::run_until_idle path).
- Two processes with independent map allocations: confirms .11.32's
  de-TLS is consumed correctly (no MAP_BUILDER cross-contamination).

## Risks

- Cooperative-only means a tight loop without yield can monopolize
  the scheduler. v1 acceptable; preemption (reductions counter) is a
  follow-up.
- Mailbox + receive (.19.3) introduces scheduler ↔ process interaction
  (block / wake); design here must accommodate the State transitions.
- Multi-thread story: Scheduler::step takes `&mut Process` borrowed
  from `&mut self.procs`. Going multi-thread requires switching to
  `Arc<Mutex<Process>>` or similar; design choice deferred but
  flagged here.

## Out of scope

- spawn / self / pid (.19.2)
- Mailbox + send / receive (.19.3)
- Per-process GC (.19.4 — verification on top of .11.31)
- Multi-threaded scheduler (follow-up)
- Preemption via reductions counter (follow-up)
title: Process / scheduler infrastructure (process = continuation ptr)
